title: Test a raw-categorical CatBoost branch with combination CTRs
summary: The dataset has very high-cardinality categorical structure (`Driver`, `Race`, `Year`, `Compound`) and the current best code mostly compresses that into frequencies and medians.
rationale: The dataset has very high-cardinality categorical structure (`Driver`, `Race`, `Year`, `Compound`) and the current best code mostly compresses that into frequencies and medians. CatBoost is explicitly designed to build ordered target statistics and feature combinations without naive one-hot leakage.
implementation_hint: Train a CatBoost classifier on the strongest numeric base features plus raw categorical columns and a small number of explicit combos like `Race_Year`, `Driver x Compound`, and `Driver x Stint`. Keep preprocessing minimal; compare against the current frequency/median engineered version under the same grouped CV.
expected_effect: May recover interaction signal from rare drivers, race-specific behavior, and compound-choice patterns that the manual encodings flatten away.
risk: If CV is not group-safe, ordered CTRs can still look deceptively strong. CatBoost may also underperform if most of the remaining signal is temporal rather than categorical.
